2603.19591.title: Data-driven ensemble prediction of the global ocean
2603.19591.paper_summary: Data-driven models have advanced deterministic ocean forecasting, but extending machine learning to probabilistic global ocean prediction remains an open challenge. Here we introduce FuXi-ONS, the first machine-learning ensemble forecasting system for the global ocean, providing 5-day forecasts on a global 1° grid up to 365 days for sea-surface temperature, sea-surface height, subsurface temperature, salinity and ocean currents. Rather than relying on repeated integration of computationally expensive numerical models, FuXi-ONS learns physically structured perturbations and incorporates an atmospheric encoding module to stabilize long-range forecasts. Evaluated against GLORYS12 reanalysis, FuXi-ONS improves both ensemble-mean skill and probabilistic forecast quality relative to deterministic and noise-perturbed baselines, and shows competitive performance against established seasonal forecast references for SST and Niño3.4 variability, while running orders of magnitude faster than conventional ensemble systems. These results provide a strong example of machine learning advancing a core problem in ocean science, and establish a practical path toward efficient probabilistic ocean forecasting and climate risk assessment.
2603.19591.paper_summary_zh: 數據驅動模型已經推進了確定性海洋預報，但將機器學習擴展到概率性全球海洋預測仍然是一個未解的挑戰。<lb/>在這裡，我們介紹FuXi-ONS，全球首個機器學習集成預報系統，提供全球1°網格的5天預報，涵蓋海表溫度、海表高度、次表層溫度、鹽度和海洋洋流，預報時間長達365天。<lb/>FuXi-ONS不依賴於重複整合計算成本高昂的數值模型，而是學習物理結構的擾動，並結合大氣編碼模塊以穩定長期預報。<lb/>與GLORYS12再分析相比，FuXi-ONS在集成均值技能和概率預報質量上相較於確定性和噪聲擾動基準都有所改善，並且在SST和Niño3.4變異性的季節性預報參考中顯示出競爭力，同時運行速度比傳統集成系統快數個量級。<lb/>這些結果提供了一個強有力的例子，展示了機器學習在推進海洋科學核心問題方面的潛力，並建立了一條通向高效概率性海洋預報和氣候風險評估的實用途徑。
2603.19591.author: Qiusheng Huang et.al.
2603.19591.authors: Qiusheng Huang, Xiaohui Zhong, Anboyu Guo, Ziyi Peng, Lei Chen, Hao Li
